mentions.note: Hold if a serious long-term investment (52-wk low, valuations attractive, under Tata management); sell on rallies if only a trading position; BSNL dependency is the drag
Hold with patience; hope on Q3, can average on good results
mentions.detail: To a viewer stuck with 1,000 shares at Rs 650, he counselled patience. The company has large receivables; if collected, Q3 could be positive. He is encouraged by the Direct-to-Mobile concept (Prasar Bharati agreement) and 5G/6G roadmap, and notes it is a Tata company likely to reinvent. Continue holding and average a bit on good Q3 commentary/results.

mentions.note: Strongly opposes; BSNL-dependent, obsolete inventory written off
mentions.detail: He completely opposes Tejas Networks, even with fresh money. About 80% of revenue depends on BSNL where the outlook is uncertain, and last quarter they wrote off a huge amount of obsolete inventory as telecom technology keeps changing. Buy a quality telecom name like Bharti Airtel instead.

mentions.note: Earnings-call transcript gives hope the stock has bottomed; definitely do not sell.
mentions.detail: We kept thinking from 600 that the worst was over, but quarter on quarter it showed losses and the stock fell to nearly 325-330. Receivables are good and the BSNL order is well placed, though many question the dependence on one PSU. He read the earnings-call transcript carefully and said there is hope that the stock has bottomed - no doubt. As of now, definitely do not sell; hold.

mentions.note: Hold despite weakness
mentions.detail: Consistent weakness with a fall of more than 50% in recent times, but valuation is present at current levels. Hold; don't average immediately.

mentions.note: At a 59-60% loss, don't book the loss at these levels - wait a bit.
mentions.detail: At these levels he said don't book the loss - hold and wait a bit. Being at a 60% loss now and booking it means recovering that 60% again in any company becomes difficult. Separately, the company received a final PLI incentive payout of Rs 70 crores for the last financial year and the stock was up 2% today.

mentions.note: Worst is over; hold 1-1.5 yr, even Rs 1000 buyers will profit
mentions.detail: Kutumba Rao said the worst is over for Tejas, it is only a matter of time. Even before BSNL money arrived, one deal drove the stock up 60-70% off its low, showing how important that deal was. The stock will do extremely well over the long term; held another one to one-and-a-half years, even those who bought at Rs 1000 will see profits.

mentions.note: Gave big losses over a year but good developments now; next six months a game changer.
mentions.detail: Hold; needs patience. Over one year it gave big losses but good developments are happening; next six months going to be a game changer, possibly very good announcements this quarter.

mentions.note: Don't sell/book loss now; bottom likely formed, held 430; decide after the broker meet.
mentions.detail: At these levels don't sell now; bottom likely formed (held 430 after attempting 510-520), worst over; decide after the broker meet based on details.

mentions.note: Worst quarter likely behind; higher tops/bottoms, chart near all-time levels; buy on declines or hold with trailing stop ~160.
mentions.detail: HOLD. Worst quarter likely behind it; consistent higher tops/higher bottoms, chart back toward all-time levels; buy on declines, or hold with a trailing stop ~160.

mentions.note: Buy on declines or fresh entry; if holding, trailing stop-loss near 160. Higher tops/bottoms, near all-time levels.
Risen ~30% off bottom, stabilizing; trading stop
mentions.detail: In recovery mode, risen about 30% from the bottom and stabilizing. Hold, but keep a trading stop since it is in profit.

mentions.note: The worst is over; no need to sell at these levels.
mentions.detail: A caller sitting on a 42% loss asked about averaging. Kutumba Rao said the worst is over for Tejas and told him to hold. The stock is consolidating now and this quarter's result is due. There is no need to sell at these levels.

mentions.price: must cross 220-230 for a real move
mentions.note: Underperformer; hold and see the result - needs to stand above 220-230.
mentions.detail: A caller was at a 46% loss. Kutumba Rao said the result has to be seen because the stock has been such an underperformer - it showed a bit of a sizzle in between but weakness came straight back. For a real move it must cross and stand above the 220-230 levels. Last quarter's result was very disappointing and the stock fell as low as 150; if this result is better than expected, the stock could continue from here.

mentions.note: Water-treatment leader; hold despite short-term weakness
mentions.detail: A viewer bought at Rs 1,600 and is at a ~21% loss. Kutumba Rao said both this and NTPC are good stocks bought at somewhat higher rates. VA Tech Wabag has leadership in water treatment with the maximum number of patents and is globally proven; it will take time but hold.

mentions.note: Buy for long term only; value after correction
mentions.detail: Buy for the long term only, not short term. After the recent correction value appears. It is especially in an exciting industry given post-climate-change water requirements and desalination demand.

mentions.note: Global water-management leader, most patents; recommended from ~1,500; 2-3 yr could give multi-mega returns, be patient.
mentions.detail: HOLD with a trailing stop ~1,600. Globally leading water-management player with the most patents; at 52-wk highs (~1,770), recommended from ~1,500; 2-3 year horizon could give multi-mega returns — be patient.

mentions.note: Hold with trailing stop-loss ~1600. Leading water-management play; 2-3 yr horizon could give multi-mega returns.
On a 500% gain, book partially to make the holding free of cost.
No doubt at all - buy on every decline; water management theme is strong.
mentions.detail: Kutumba Rao said there is no doubt at all about VA Tech Wabag and one can buy it on every decline, because water treatment and water management companies will all do well from here. The El Nino effect showed just how important water management is. However, the stock has already risen a lot with a tremendous rally over the last year, so he said buy it only when good declines come.

mentions.note: Exit on rallies; very weak
mentions.detail: Near a 52-week low with weak December-quarter results; he advised against averaging the caller's 50% loss. Financial performance is not promising at all, so exit on rallies.

mentions.note: Wait for management confidence; too early to say the worst is over.
mentions.detail: A caller thought Syngene looked attractively valued after the correction. Kutumba Rao said he would wait, because disappointment comes on and off in Syngene and it is still difficult to say the worst is over. This time not just the result but the management commentary will be key. He would wait for management confidence rather than enter the stock immediately - and that caution is reflected in the price, which is still floundering near its 52-week low.

mentions.price: exit even on a technical rally (~Rs 60 now)
mentions.note: Don't average a badly underperforming stock - exit; corporate governance issues.
mentions.detail: A caller at a 25% loss asked about averaging. Kutumba Rao said no - one shouldn't keep averaging something that is badly underperforming, and Soma Distilleries underperforms a lot within the liquor space. He should have come out long ago: 'if you see one cockroach in the kitchen, understand there are ten more.' Since the management controversy and corporate governance issues a couple of years back, the stock has made continuous lower tops and lower bottoms down to Rs 60 today. Even if a technical rally comes or the price is operated better, it is better to exit.

unitedspiritsradicokhaitanifbagroindustr.name: United Spirits / Radico Khaitan / IFB Agro Industries

mentions.note: Stick to the leader - the only three stocks worth owning in the liquor space.
mentions.detail: Asked for an alternative in the liquor space after rejecting Soma Distilleries, Kutumba Rao said always stick to the leader. If you want to be in the liquor space there are only three stocks: United Spirits, Radico Khaitan and IFB Agro Industries. He named them as the leaders rather than giving individual entry levels.

mentions.note: Six-month festival/wedding cycle; better retail names exist.
mentions.detail: AVOID. Six-month festival/wedding cycle; better retail names exist.

mentions.note: Avoidable stock - weak result with falling profits, as flagged many times.
mentions.detail: Kutumba Rao noted he has said many times to avoid Sai Silks and Kala Mandir type companies. In line with that, the stock was weak in the market yesterday and the result showed profits falling. He called these avoidable stocks and said that should be remembered.

mentions.note: Positional F&O trade for the June series only; stop-loss ~450 per risk profile.
mentions.detail: BUY (positional F&O trade, June series only; stop-loss ~450 per your risk profile).

mentions.note: Surprising weak fall on rumours; the time to observe the company has come.
mentions.detail: Kutumba Rao flagged a surprising weak fall in Patanjali yesterday amid various rumours. With the weakness of the last three sessions the stock has now come below 350. He said one has to observe carefully what is happening in the company - the time to observe has come.

mentions.note: Hold 670-690; result in line, low PE (~22); consolidating after result spike.
mentions.detail: Result came in line with expectations; consolidates after the result spike; hold between 670-690; low PE (~22) even now.

mentions.note: Very good result with profit up ~40%; possible slow recovery.
mentions.detail: Kutumba Rao said he himself had thought HDB Financial Services was an underperformer, but the result was very good because profit rose almost 40%. On that basis he said maybe one can look at whether HDB Financial Services could stage a slow recovery.

mentions.note: Don't buy at all - CERC norms will cut margins a lot next year.
mentions.detail: A viewer wanted to enter IEX because the subsidiary Indian Gas Exchange IPO is coming. Kutumba Rao said that in fact if the stock rose at IPO time he was thinking of saying exit, while the viewer is thinking of buying. A short-term positional rally may come then, but the stock has become weak, and the CERC norms all start implementation in the coming year, after which margins will also fall a lot. It is not a great stock to hold as of now - avoid it, don't buy IEX at all.

mentions.note: Asked with L&T for a Rs 25,000 investment; add slowly in the dips for the long term, in a staggered manner rather than in one go.
Clear-cut outperformer; buyable at current levels
mentions.detail: He said Jio looked like a clear-cut outperformer on both 1-week and 3-4-week performance, and can be invested in at current levels for a one-year-plus horizon.

mentions.note: Hold for the long term.
mentions.detail: HOLD (long term).

mentions.note: Don't average; the result is today - decide only after seeing it.
mentions.detail: A viewer at a 25% loss asked whether to average or sell. Kutumba Rao said don't average. He pointed out the result is due today itself, so a decision can only be taken after seeing the result, and deferred the call to the next day's show.

mentions.note: Certainly can buy; capacity expansion will show up in the numbers.
mentions.detail: The same viewer was thinking of buying Valectra (name as heard in the captions). Kutumba Rao said certainly she can buy. Good expansion is happening in the company, and once the capacity expansion fully comes into availability, a lot of improvement will be visible in the numbers. Results are also due soon, in 10-15 days, so take a look then.

mentions.note: New facility opening; slowly gaining strength.
mentions.detail: Azad Engineering could be active with a new facility opening. He said Azad is slowly gaining strength, and noted this was also mentioned in the recent workshop. The anchor later compared it to Sansera as a high-quality precision engineering company.

mentions.note: Hold with SL ~1500; richly valued PE ~90
mentions.detail: Caller bought near the peak (~2,000) after a ~1,000 rise in a month. Hold with a stop-loss around 1,500; if the stock rebounds, exit close to his price. It is a good stock without doubt but very richly valued at about PE 90.

mentions.note: Decent stock; trail a stop-loss if in profit - it's results season.
mentions.detail: A viewer holding 20 shares of Azad Engineering at 1,350 asked whether to hold or sell. Kutumba Rao said they are all decent stocks and that he had bought decent stocks. He noted it wasn't clear whether the viewer is in profit or loss, but if in profit he would put a trailing stop-loss because it is results season.

mentions.note: Hold; still steam
mentions.detail: For now hold both this and TD Power; there is still steam in both stocks.

mentions.note: Decent stock; trail a stop-loss if in profit - it's results season.
mentions.detail: Part of the same viewer's three-stock question. Kutumba Rao grouped Rossell Techsys with the others as a decent stock that was well bought. Without knowing whether the viewer is in profit or loss, his advice was to hold, and if in profit to put a trailing stop-loss given it is results season.

mentions.note: Hold at ~40% loss; summer demand and electronics/domestic-manufacturing focus over next 1-2 quarters; not a stock to sell now.
mentions.detail: Hold for now; we're moving into summer so demand can rise again, and focus on electronics/domestic manufacturing increases over next 1-2 quarters. Not a stock to sell at this time. If bought for trading, book profit on recovery/rallies.

mentions.note: Hold for now; summer demand rising and domestic electronics manufacturing focus; stock showed resilience, not one to sell here.
Buy only with medium-to-long-term view; corrected sharply recently; standard manner.
mentions.detail: Buy only if you have a medium-to-long-term view; doubt significant short-term returns; corrected sharply recently; buy only in a standard/staggered manner.

mentions.note: Recovery likely; ~550 if this quarter's results are good.
mentions.detail: 100 shares @ 50. Recovery chances even though entered at a high; not far off, ~550 will come, probably if this time's results are good.

mentions.note: High volatility / sharp moves last 6 months; decide after results.
mentions.detail: HOLD. High volatility / sharp moves the last six months; decide after results.

mentions.note: 5-star business and stock with lots of momentum; hold long term, act if the level breaks.
mentions.detail: HOLD (long term). A "5-star business and 5-star stock" with lots of momentum; take it day by day, act if the level breaks.

mentions.note: Good EMS company but looks weak; wait for Q1 commentary, consider switching to Cine SJS.
mentions.detail: PG Electroplast is doing well in the EMS space but struggles to sustain above its prior top after crashes (fell ~800 to 500 last Sep/Oct and keeps getting sold near 800, then down to 600). Recovery does not look fast. He suggested waiting for the Q1 commentary before deciding, said the stock looks weak, and noted one could have shifted into Cine SJS (already up).

mentions.note: Decent stock; trail a stop-loss if in profit - it's results season.
mentions.detail: Part of the same viewer's three-stock question. Kutumba Rao said these are all decent stocks that were decently bought. His advice was to hold, and if the viewer is in profit, to put a trailing stop-loss because it is results season.

mentions.note: Positional buy up to the NSE IPO
mentions.detail: Result hit it out of the park and the stock is at an all-time high. As a positional trade up to the NSE IPO, he said BSE will sustain and to buy it. A dip toward 3,000 (gap-fill) is a good buy point.

mentions.note: Fully priced; wait for the NSE IPO
mentions.detail: Said BSE is fully priced and there is no need to buy before the NSE IPO; wait for that.

mentions.note: Capital-market proxy; next big move likely with the NSE IPO.
mentions.detail: 600 shares @ 2,455. Capital-market-related stock (good when markets are good). With the NSE IPO coming and uncertainty over how it lists post-crash, the next big move would come only with the NSE IPO.

mentions.note: Hold/buy small for long term; some profit booking possible once NSE also lists.
Just wait - the NSE will be there; hold for now rather than buy fresh.
mentions.detail: A viewer asked about buying BSE along with CDSL, SMS Pharma and Natco Pharma. On BSE, Kutumba Rao said one can just wait because the NSE will be there, and to hold BSE for now. He gave a clear preference for CDSL over a fresh BSE purchase.

mentions.note: Hold; stable depository business, no competition (only NSDL and CDSL).
mentions.detail: For a viewer holding at 1493 (at a loss), he says hold. Whatever IPO rally happens, depository companies have a stable business with no competition - only NSDL and CDSL exist. Even without steady growth it is a steady business model; once costs are cut and profits shown better, there is scope for the stock to rise.

mentions.note: Sell to raise cash rather than book larger Infosys loss
mentions.detail: For a caller needing money who held CDSL (~Rs 5,000 loss) and Infosys (~Rs 40,000 loss), Kutumba Rao said sell CDSL now, because booking the much larger Infosys loss is not right; he also suggested averaging Infosys. (Ramakrishna gave the opposite view, favouring selling Infosys.)

mentions.note: Not a great choice
mentions.detail: Asked about investing in CDSL now, Kutumba Rao said it is not a great choice. He cautioned to follow technicals carefully given the weak market.

mentions.note: Long-term buy; steady capital-markets business, no problem
mentions.detail: In the same answer (Waaree Energy and CDSL, targeting 100% in five years), Kutumba Rao said CDSL is fine for the long term: capital markets keep generating volumes and new listings, and CDSL has a steady (paid) business with no problem. He judged a 5-year 100% return possible.

mentions.note: Medium term; will recover.
mentions.detail: HOLD (medium term); will recover.

mentions.note: Start accumulating; duopoly sector and likely to outperform NSDL.
mentions.detail: Kutumba Rao said to start accumulating CDSL, calling it a good share for the long term. There are only two companies in this space for us - NSDL and CDSL - making it a duopoly sector. Between the two, he said the chances are visible that CDSL will outperform NSDL.

mentions.note: At highs; wait for a significant correction before buying
mentions.detail: Already at highs, so watch carefully. Momentum is good and it has performed well over a period, but he would not buy at current levels; be wait-and-watch and buy only on a significant correction.

mentions.note: Fully priced this month; wait for a significant correction, accumulate near 17,000.
mentions.detail: Asked whether Force Motors can be taken at this level after its big rally and small correction, Kutumba Rao said this month he wouldn't advise it because the stock is fully priced. He would wait for a significant correction, though whether the correction comes to that extent can't be said. It has already come from around 25-26,000 to 18,380; maybe close to 17,000 one can start accumulating. There is no hurry with such a high-priced stock.

mentions.note: Wait a quarter; July 15 record date already over - calculate the actual distribution yield.
mentions.detail: A viewer holding 25,000 units bought at Rs 76 asked whether to hold or sell and when the dividend comes. Kutumba Rao said it is a hybrid instrument that listed only recently at what he thought was too high a price, and it has corrected continuously since to near its 52-week low - he too had thought that price wouldn't sustain. He would wait a quarter. The company had announced a record date of July 15 for the distribution, which is already over. In these InvITs the return on capital starts at just 1.7-2.1%; going by the details he was seeing it looked like about 1.12 per unit, roughly an 11-11.2% return, which is as good as a good dividend yield. He said to calculate the actual yield on the Rs 76 cost once the distribution comes and compare it year-on-year rather than answering off the cuff.

mentions.note: Would pick it from the list, but not the time to pyramid/add now
mentions.detail: From a viewer's large-cap list for pyramiding, Reliance was one of the two he'd choose. But he stressed the market is not trending upward and this is not the time to pyramid; pyramiding should wait until Q3 results give earnings visibility and the market moves up. Be very cautious pyramiding in a flat market.

mentions.note: Add slowly on every dip in this range; a no-brainer stock
mentions.detail: Asked about buying for a 1-5+ year horizon given value unlocking, he said one can add slowly here with no trouble. In this price range, keep adding slowly whenever a dip comes. He called it a no-brainer stock.

mentions.note: Accumulate slowly for the long term on sum-of-the-parts and AI value
mentions.detail: Kutumba Rao said the US $300 billion investment is not the reason to buy Reliance. The case is long-term sum-of-the-parts value (Jio divestment/possible IPO, retail) and AI developments relevant here that will have more impact on the stock. He said start accumulating slowly because it is somewhat resilient versus several other stocks, but with a long-term horizon.

mentions.note: Add slowly / accumulate on dips; currently in consolidation and will participate when a large-cap rally comes.

Has corrected somewhat recently and the Reliance Jio listing is tomorrow. Large caps are underperforming; if foreign portfolio investors come back there is scope to outperform. For the long term - five to ten years - definitely hold.

Results tomorrow are the main trigger; positive results can be expected with the O2C business having scope to be very strong and high crude lifting GRMs. Expectations are high because of the Jio IPO. FPIs/institutions are quite underweight, so there is scope to raise weightage and bring buying interest in the short-to-medium term. One reason the index is stuck near 24,000 is that support is not coming from Reliance; a heavyweight beat plus Jio IPO guidance could break the range.
Hold; one of top-3 power cos, scope to 250 once 182 crosses
mentions.detail: Hold Adani Power, no need to hurry as it is one of the top-three power companies. The recent correction eliminated weaker players in the stock. Once it crosses the recent high of 182, there is scope to go up to 250 on the way. Definitely hold for the next one year.

mentions.note: Hold; no need to be scared
mentions.detail: Hold Adani Power; no need to be scared much. He added that power companies have good times due to data-center demand, and viewed Trump's rate-payer protection pledge for AI data centers as a positive for the space globally.

mentions.note: One of his two explicit long-term power picks
mentions.detail: Asked to name two power stocks in his view, he said his choice would be Adani Power and Tata Power, calling them long-term, low-hype counters. He warned against blindly chasing hot momentum power names (Atlanta Electricals, Quality Power, High Voltage), advising small position sizes and no over-allocation to one sector.

mentions.note: Risen extraordinarily, out of allegations; hold for now with high risk appetite.
Good power-generation company (compare with JSW Energy); invest equally with CG Power.
mentions.detail: BUY. Good company (power generation); compare with JSW Energy, not CG Power. Invest equally with CG Power.

mentions.note: His power-sector pick for a caller investing Rs 1 lakh.
mentions.detail: The caller had already studied the power sector to some extent and the amount involved was small, so Kutumba Rao named Adani Power on the power side. This sat alongside the anchor's separate suggestion that the caller simply add to the CG Power and Siemens Energy India holdings he already had.

mentions.note: First choice in EV/two-wheeler space; captured the market, better bet even in declines.
mentions.detail: For the EV space, first choice is TVS Motors, because in two-wheelers they've captured the market; better bet even in declines. Ola/Ather can be looked at; watch for a turnaround.

mentions.note: Add in tick lines; auto-ancillaries/autos turning active again.
mentions.detail: Add in tick lines.

mentions.note: His automobile pick for the same Rs 1 lakh caller.
mentions.detail: Named straight away as the buy on the automobile side. Kranthi endorsed it separately as a silent wealth creator worth buying on dips, citing its 7-8 year price chart and its repeated mention at their workshops.

mentions.note: Buy on dips. The stock to look forward to, as Kutumba Rao said - a silent wealth creator over the last 7-8 years, visible in its price chart; suggested at many of their workshops. He grouped M&M and Maruti Suzuki as great companies too.
EV two-wheeler; very resilient. Add slowly on dips; petrol price hikes post-election could boost EV space.
Add slowly on dips; strong stock even in this market.
mentions.detail: Add slowly on dips; a very strong stock even in the current market.

mentions.note: Prefers Ather over Ola as of now
mentions.detail: Asked Ola vs Ather (viewer Surya), Kutumba Rao's preference is Ather as of now. Ola may see a short-term move but Ather looks fundamentally better. On Ola, wait for results before gaining confidence since management always speaks bullishly.

mentions.note: Recent listing, strong, holding above 1,000; hold.
mentions.detail: Recent listing; performing strongly, holding above 1,000 since listing; hold.

mentions.note: EV two-wheeler leader; shaping up nicely, can be added.
mentions.detail: Kutumba Rao said Ather too can be added for the automobile allocation - a good, beautiful two-wheeler electric company. In his market view he called it the leader in EV with no need for any doubt, noting Hero MotoCorp is investing a fresh Rs 1,000 crore on top of its existing ~29% stake, and that the stock is already trading near a record high.

mentions.note: Can be added in the EV space, but it has risen a lot and valuations aren't that cheap. These are futuristic stocks - add slowly on dips.
Buy for short/short-medium term only, not long term
mentions.detail: The result has come and is good, but the stock is not a fancy one. Keeping that in view, it can be bought for the short or short-medium term. Long-term bets in such companies do not work well.

mentions.note: Under-performing badly among NBFCs; don't focus on a non-performer.
mentions.detail: A relatively new listing with only about one and a half to two years of history. Results are good with improvement visible from time to time and stable margins around 36-37%, but the stock has fallen from 130 to about 90-92 since the leadership change and is under-performing badly among NBFC stocks. When a stock isn't performing, giving it all your attention is unnecessary when names like Shriram Finance have given wonderful returns. Avoid it.

mentions.note: Preferred over Piramal Pharma for the long term
mentions.detail: Asked whether to buy Piramal Pharma or Laurus Labs for the long term, he said 'I would buy Laurus only.' It has held very strongly, one of the few stocks firm even in this week's weak market, and looks relatively safe unless the market falls a lot.

mentions.note: At 52-week high; consistent recent uptrend from 800 to 1000; valuations not cheap but management guidance good; add on dips at different price points.
Momentum in the stock, next 1000 level holds; book a little profit at higher range if needed, but a stock to hold.
Good results, holding above 1000; book if bought just before results
mentions.detail: Results were good. The stock is holding above 1,000 and corrected after touching its 52-week high of 1,141. Those who bought just before the results (after the last quarter) can book profit, only that - there's nothing to be afraid of. The CDMO segment disappointed a little quarter-on-quarter.

mentions.note: Switch target from PPL Pharma - no doubt.
mentions.detail: He endorses shifting from PPL Pharma into Laurus, saying there is no doubt about that switch.

mentions.note: Accumulate in the 920-950 support zone
mentions.detail: Repeatedly dips below 1,000 (965; 930-940 in early Feb). Best entry as of now is the last three-to-six-month support zone; advised accumulating around 920 to 950.

mentions.note: Definitely buy in this correction
mentions.detail: He said Laurus Labs is definitely a buy in this correction, with results that keep appearing better and better. He grouped it with Natco as a buy.

mentions.note: Hold; Q4 results will be good, stock very resilient with no selling.
mentions.detail: Q4 results will be good (excellent numbers), nothing objectionable. The stock is very resilient and doesn't flinch; pharma broadly strong/defensive. Can be held.

mentions.note: Very resilient even in market fall; no selling; Q4 results expected good though valuations a bit high. Can be held.
Buy after today's result, especially on a dip; portfolio stock
mentions.detail: Trading ~1,106 vs an all-time high of 1,144. 'Buy on rumours, sell on news' means some profit-booking is possible, but he is not expecting a bad result; rising capex (new Vizag/Nektar project) will weigh on net profit. He calls it a portfolio stock that could rise another 50-60% in 8 months on a one-year view; buy after the result, and buy more if it falls.

mentions.note: His first choice for CDMO / overseas-exposure theme
mentions.detail: When discussing CDMO and overseas-exposure ideas, Kutumba Rao said his first choice would be Laurus. He presented it ahead of his second choice (Sai Parenterals) as a preferred play on the CDMO story.

mentions.note: Big potential; medium-to-long-term hold; target ~1,600-1,700. Book only for FOMO short term.
mentions.detail: Never said sell in 1.5 years; continuously buy. Higher PE now but performance stabilizing q-o-q, decent profits even after capex; into a bigger orbit (~Rs 1-lakh-cr m-cap, ~1,600-1,700) once capex done. Book profits only for extreme short-term/FOMO; medium-to-long-term hold.

mentions.note: Viewer query; hold.
First pharma choice, always, no doubt.
mentions.detail: BUY. First pharma choice "always, no doubt."

mentions.note: High-conviction; buy partial now instead of waiting for lower level
mentions.detail: In stocks with strong conviction, buy about 25% upfront rather than insisting on an exact entry level. The awaited 1100 never came; it is already at ~1400. Start buying some now and add in staggered fashion.

mentions.note: Do NOT sell to switch into CG Power; let profits run (~200% up). Use fresh capital instead.
mentions.detail: Do NOT sell to switch into CG Power. Learn to let profits run (in ~200% profit). Interchanging good stocks unnecessarily is pointless; CG Power is also a good stock — use fresh capital rather than swapping.

mentions.note: Never sell; strong pharma name.
mentions.detail: Holder is up ~6x from 292. 'The word sell will never come from my mouth for Laurus.' Pharma is strong on a firm dollar; Laurus is up ~10% over the last month and crossed 1,500.

mentions.note: Up 150% but no need to book profits; Q1 results expected to be good - sell only if tired of waiting.
The pharma leg of the approved combination; accumulate slowly on dips - a good combination overall.
His preferred CDMO/CRDMO name; buy on every decline.

For a company posting quarterly profit above Rs 2,000 crore, a ~Rs 20 crore SEBI notice is nothing; the company is doing well, so hold.

mentions.note: Holdable ~1 year as environment turns positive, but high-risk; use a trailing stop-loss.
mentions.detail: HOLD for about a year given the positive environment, but there's risk; with high risk appetite, hold carefully with a trailing stop-loss.

mentions.note: Hold for now and watch this quarter's results; the stock is coming into the limelight again after six months to a year of consolidation. If the results are in any way disappointing, exit on rallies and go into Vedanta Power.
Hold; buy near 355, stabilizing 360-370
mentions.detail: After the recent result it corrected and is stabilizing between 360-370. Definitely hold; buy too when it comes closer to 355.

mentions.note: Hold; company doing well, dairy is seasonal
mentions.detail: Quarterly result was slightly flat; stock stabilized around 430-440 and also dipped to 360-370. Company is doing extremely well and the product portfolio is diversifying. Dairy is seasonal and procurement-rate changes cause dips, but no need to fear. Hold.

mentions.note: No need to be afraid at all; a very attractive bet after this correction.
mentions.detail: Last three quarters' results are seen, the company is now literally debt free, expansions are already complete and many new products have been launched (Heritage Nu and other nutrition-based products through two or three subsidiaries), making it more than a plain milk-and-butter seller. Dairy shares have been flattish for a year to a year and a half on higher procurement prices, but that is a seasonal effect, and with the EU, US and New Zealand deals done it is clear the Indian dairy industry is not being impacted. Buy at current levels with a medium-to-long-term view, not for a week or a month. He separately rejected the Sakshi allegations tying Heritage to Indapur Dairy and Bole Baba Dholpur Dairy as baseless.

mentions.note: Buy/accumulate from current levels for the long term (>1 year hold).
mentions.detail: Same caller query. He said both stocks (Sezzle India and Heritage Foods) should be bought/accumulated from current levels for the long term, holding a minimum of more than one year; they can give decent returns.

mentions.note: Invest, no second thoughts
mentions.detail: The stock is exactly at its support level now. He says definitely invest the planned Rs 50,000, no second thoughts.

mentions.note: Correction is a good buying opportunity; accumulate 320-350
mentions.detail: Said the recent correction is giving a good opportunity to buy and there is no need to panic. All dairy stocks benefit now that import/trade-deal fears (NZ, US, EU) have passed; efficient low-debt dairy companies will rise. One of the better bets to buy; at current levels it corrects and accumulates between 320 to 350.

mentions.note: Milk prices rising, ice-cream season strong
mentions.detail: Milk prices are jumping and the ice-cream season is running well, so accumulate at this rate (~322). Patience is needed; some slow results improvement may be seen next quarter.

mentions.note: Ice-cream expansion went well and focus on value-added products; a long-term player from here.
Start accumulating; no major dip expected
mentions.detail: His own stock. Start accumulating - no major dip expected from here as it is already in consolidation mode.

mentions.note: Stock is in consolidation after good outperformance two years back. New value-added products (Heritage ice creams) introduced last quarter give a chance of margins rising. It is a multi-dimensional business, unlike the standalone ice-cream business of Quality Walls.
Buy nearer 22-23, in March
mentions.detail: HUL-demerged ice-cream entity, double down-circuit; market questions the ~Rs 6,000 cr market cap for an ice-cream company. Buyable closer to 22-23 levels, in March, with the summer season approaching.

mentions.note: Has fully bought the Netherlands' Magnum business from Hindustan Unilever and is now Magnum's holding company; strong performance in the stock. But it is by and large a standalone ice-cream business, versus multi-dimensional Heritage - switch from Heritage only if you have the risk appetite.
If the caller wants Vedanta Power, build a separate position in it rather than switching out. If Suzlon's results this quarter disappoint, exit Suzlon on rallies and move into Vedanta Power.
Fundamentally sound but bought high; don't average
mentions.detail: A fundamentally sound defence stock, but the caller bought very high near the 52-week high (2,464). Don't average now; do fresh buying only after the result, maybe closer to about 1,400 where it would trade at an attractive ~45 PE. Until then, hold.

mentions.note: Among defense stocks (mostly fairly priced after rallies), attractive on valuations; add slowly on dips.
Defense still attractive; near-term pause but long-term outperformance scope; put on watch list.

Definitely hold for the long term, and accumulate in this price range for a long-term view. Recent OFS was subscribed by very strong companies though some underperformance is visible. The medium-to-short-term overhang on all PSUs is that the government sells stake at a discount as soon as the stock performs, to clear its deficit.
Wait and watch; treasury-loss risk on yields
mentions.detail: As of now, wait and watch. As yields rise, treasury losses for banks could also rise going forward.

mentions.note: Hold. Positivity is coming into the banking sector. The stock has corrected from around 326 to the 250 range and looks attractive here for the medium to long term.
Hold and add on declines; better-performing PSU bank
mentions.detail: Definitely hold and add on declines. Average whenever it comes to Rs 140 and below as it is one of the better-performing PSU banks. It was recommended strongly cum-dividend earlier around 90.

mentions.note: Large dividend payer; PSU banks slightly high now, so add on dips.
mentions.detail: He said banks are slightly on the higher side as of now; if they fall a bit, large dividend payers like Canara Bank can be attempted on the dip for dividend yield above the FD level.

mentions.note: Bought ~Rs 100; fine, no problem, hold.
mentions.detail: Viewer holds Canara Bank bought at ~Rs 100 with a long-term view. Kutumba Rao: that's fine, no problem, hold.

mentions.note: Among SBI/Canara/Kotak: moderate/high-beta pick if taking a bit of risk
Buy on declines, not now; June-ending could be weak (treasury yields fell, large PSU treasury books).
mentions.detail: Buy on declines, not now. Results maybe a bit better but June-ending could be weak; treasury yields fell and PSU banks hold large treasury portfolios — buy on a dip, no rush.

mentions.note: Bank stocks stable but entry timing is key
mentions.detail: Asked about Canara Bank and Siemens Energy, he said both can be added on declines. PSU banks that corrected were recovering; bank stocks are stable but the time of entry is key, and he would accumulate Canara closer to 130 or below 130.

mentions.note: Hold for dividend then exit on a 5-10% rally
mentions.detail: For a caller ~15% down with a 6-month view, he says hold for the dividend and exit on a small rally. Canara Bank historically rises ~10% post ex-dividend; with ~3% dividend yield, the stock still needs ~12% to recover the 15% loss.

mentions.note: Good stock; add carefully, no hurry
mentions.detail: He said all three of the caller's stocks are good and you can add, but there is no hurry given market weakness. Buy but watch the entry point carefully.

mentions.note: Corrected ~20-25% (160+ -> 130); accumulate slowly, wait for RBI policy.
mentions.detail: ACCUMULATE slowly. Corrected ~20-25% (160+ → 130); wait for the RBI credit policy first, no hurry.

mentions.note: Narrow 125-145 band at ~6.5 PE; needs patience, should rally and cross 160 again.
mentions.detail: corrected from 160 to ~135, narrow 125–145 band (like most PSU banks); at ~6.5 PE. Hold, needs patience; should rally and cross 160 again.

mentions.note: Hold, along with Bank of Baroda, on the back of returning positivity in the banking sector.

For the long term it can be held. A strong correction has been seen, but on a last-10-years basis Trent is an outperformer. A long-term investor can definitely hold and add on dips - it's a lifestyle stock.
Some revival after touching bottom; hold for now.
mentions.detail: For now I'd say hold Trent, because we're seeing some revival in the stock after it touched bottom.

mentions.note: Hold for the medium to long term; wait for this quarter's results. A moderate correction is on, with the quarter's business updates impacting the stock negatively, but a rebound is possible from this range. All Tata Group shares are consolidating. Discretionary spending is rising and the Zudio brand is a key revenue generator with growing tier-one/tier-two penetration.

A Maruti associated group company that has rallied from almost 100 - a good company to buy on dips after seeing this quarter's results. Enter based on how the quarterly results come, not on the price movement. A strict stop-loss is a must for any trading, since the stock has risen 100% in just one-two months and these stocks tend to rally then go into long consolidation.

Hold at this price range and watch this quarter's results; not for a fresh entry. The stock is very strong and resilient but is seeing profit booking after a recent all-time high of 2,600. Strong support at Rs 2,000 - keep that as a trailing stop-loss and hold the position.
Not a great result - only modest top-line growth, though net profit jumped 200%. But one can't judge from a single quarter's result; wait and look at the Q4 numbers before deciding.
Momentum intact at a 52-week high; hold with a trailing stop loss.
mentions.detail: A stock that was at 110-120 until recently went up to 300 in just a month and is now consolidating at 280-285 even after the recent correction, at a 52-week high. He asked the caller whether the buy was short, medium or long term. If playing short-term momentum, compulsorily keep a trailing stop loss; if playing for the medium term, keep the stop somewhat more distant and hold the positions for now.

mentions.note: Jumped and is back in consolidation at a 52-week high; hold with a trailing stop.
mentions.detail: Like Standard Engineering, Capstone jumped and from that jump is going back into consolidation mode, and is also at a 52-week high. There is short-term momentum in both stocks. For a momentum play keep a compulsory trailing stop loss; for a medium-term view keep a wider stop and hold as of now.

mentions.note: Not on a weak wicket - extremely strong; can be bought fresh at lower levels.
mentions.detail: The caller holds 500 shares at 870. Astra Microwave is one of the better electronic companies catering to the defence space and has the maximum number of microwave and micro-electronic scientists working in-house. The stock is not on a weak wicket - it is extremely strong - and has only corrected about 20% from its 52-week high in the recent fall, with recent results good (top line up, bottom line up). It can even be bought fresh at lower levels, with a buying point somewhere around 860-850.

mentions.note: Definitely a buy; one of the better micro-electronics and defence-electronics names.
mentions.detail: Astra is definitely one of the better companies in micro-electronics and defence electronics, trading at its 52-week high, and the last quarter result was extremely good. The demerger announcement and the board meeting are already done, so whether buying post-demerger unlocks further value and returns is difficult to say immediately. Even so he said definitely a buy, and there is definitely no need to sell Astra Micro.

mentions.note: Decent MNC but long-term-only returns; no extraordinary wealth creation.
mentions.detail: AstraZeneca Pharma is a multinational company without doubt and a decent company he would not say no to, but it only gives returns in the long term - one should not expect great returns in the short term. It is a high-priced stock trading above almost 8,000 (a Rs 2 paid-up share). A steady-performance company, but not one where extraordinary wealth creation will happen.

mentions.note: Exit on rallies; recent fraud hits this quarter, but slightly better/investment-grade of the three.
mentions.detail: IDFC has its own problems; the recent fraud will negatively impact this quarter's earnings season, so exit on rallies. Among the three it is the slightly better stock and can be viewed somewhat as investment-grade.

mentions.note: Kranti: decent stock, can be held at present (caller bought at 72).
Exit (held 2 yrs, 5% loss) and switch into GMR Airports / Jio Finance.
mentions.detail: Caller holding 2 years at 5% loss; Kutumba Rao endorsed exiting and switching into the better stocks GMR Airports / Jio Finance.

mentions.note: Recovering after the Haryana fraud; Warburg Pincus gets majority equity by next September.
mentions.detail: He had strongly called IDFC out as an investment idea long ago near 30-40 and the stock doubled. A fraud in Haryana - a nexus with the state government and corrupt bank employees - then knocked it from near 200 to below 60. It is slowly recovering and is back to 80-plus with the 52-week high nearby at 87. Warburg Pincus and other major investors get majority equity by next September, which will make it more like a multinational bank than a domestic bank. Hold.

mentions.price: 27; book profits in the mid-30s to 40
mentions.note: The worst is over; textiles improving - hold with patience and book profits in the mid-30s to 40.
mentions.detail: Ex-rights the stock corrected below 20 and has since been slowly recovering, now stabilising at 27. In his view the worst is over in the stock, so hold it. Textile companies are also slowly improving, so with a little patience the stock should get back into the mid-30s to 40 [as heard], at which point profits can be booked.

mentions.note: Recent activity may not sustain; hold with a trading stop around 80.
mentions.detail: Activity increased recently but whether it sustains continuously is difficult to say - from around 60-65 on June 1 the stock went almost to 85-95 all at once and is now consolidating at 90. He would keep a trading stop loss and hold. The trading stop is slightly far away at 78, but even at those levels, around 80, keep a trading stop and hold.

mentions.note: Hold for dividend; target rates far off
mentions.detail: For a viewer holding NHPC (bought ~100) and IRFC (~175), he said enjoy the dividend and keep holding; the target rates will take a lot of time to come.

mentions.note: Underperformer, but hold as a steady large-dividend player with a good yield.
mentions.detail: NHPC is definitely something of an underperformer: it went above 100 last year, corrected below 70, and is now stabilising at 80. A weak monsoon always affects stocks like NHPC because, despite diversified green power, hydro is ultimately the maximum revenue generator, so mild weakness is possible. But there is a regular large dividend play with a good yield, so instead of writing it off as an underperformer, hold it as a steady dividend player. It should not be sold at this rate.

mentions.note: Recommends; demerger value-unlock, only 20 PE
mentions.detail: Already up since he flagged it as an investment idea. The demerger opens a value-unlock possibility and it trades at only ~20 PE, so he would recommend Vedanta as of now. Expects some outperformance.

mentions.note: Accumulate near 640-650; demerger likely within 6 months
mentions.detail: He said the Vedanta demerger issue looks set to be completed within the next six months, so one can buy. The correction in non-ferrous metals will not be prolonged because renewable-driven demand will revive non-ferrous metals; Vedanta is one of the larger players. Whenever it closes around Rs 640-650 the stock should be accumulated.

mentions.note: Good dividend but runs at the promoter's whim; avoid for peace of mind.
mentions.detail: He said Vedanta gives a good dividend now, but the company runs at the promoter's whim, so leave such risky bets aside; for peace of mind the PSU dividend stocks are a better opportunity.

mentions.note: Add slowly before the demerger record date; post-demerger add again based on resulting entities (e.g. silver).
mentions.detail: Record date for the demerger has come; add slowly before the record date. After the demerger completes, add again depending on the resulting different companies (e.g. silver).

mentions.note: Looks a bit weak; demerger issue pending. Hold with caution.
mentions.detail: Vedanta looks a bit weak; there's a demerger issue too. Treated as hold-with-caution alongside the decent companies.

mentions.note: Viewer holds 150 shares at 495 into the demerger; asked if his earlier view had changed, he said for now hold Vedanta.
Buy in weakness for 2-3 yr view; demerger to Vedanta juniors, sum-of-parts decent returns; high dividend payer.
mentions.detail: Buy in this weakness for a 2-3 year view. Corporate restructuring/demerger to form 'Vedanta juniors'; on sum-of-the-parts basis decent-returns possibility. High dividend payer; governance concerns aside, hasn't given investors losses.

mentions.note: Buy/add; small negative (fire-accident court matters) will pass; restructuring + demerger ahead; big commodity player.
mentions.detail: Buy / can add. Small negative today (court matters from the fire accident) will pass over time; more corporate restructuring and a demerger exercise ahead; one of the bigger commodity players amid a strengthening commodity rally.

mentions.note: Buy only on dips below 300 / in 260-280; now a dividend play
mentions.detail: Recently demerged and now trading standalone. Treat it largely as a dividend play, since all the important units have been demerged. If at all you want to buy, buy below 300, or in the 260-280 band closer to it.

mentions.price: target 330-340 if the metal cycle turns
mentions.note: Post-demerger the stock is at true value but has gone extremely low; hold.
mentions.detail: After the demerger the stock has come to its true value, but it is at low levels and corrected recently to extremely low levels. If the metal cycle turns again, the stock will easily reach 330-340 levels too. Hold. (The caller's other stock, 'First Fintech', was not audible and went unanswered.)

mentions.note: Hold with extended long-term view
mentions.detail: Described it as a market proxy that tracks broad swings (e.g. Adani stocks). Now a consistent high-dividend payer, so hold, but with an extended long-term view.

mentions.note: Only for extreme long term; accumulate staggered. Spectacular quarterly result.
mentions.detail: Only for the extreme long term; accumulate in a staggered manner. Subdued since listing but gave a spectacular quarterly result, a reflection of capital markets. Not for 1-3 months.

mentions.note: NSE stake ~Rs 5,400 cr (=10% of LIC mkt cap); signs of +10% once NSE issue factors into NAV.
mentions.detail: Speculative-longs / watch list. NSE stake value alone is ~Rs 5,400 cr, which is 10% of LIC's market cap; once the NSE issue happens that gets factored into NAV, so LIC has signs of rising 10%. Stock may go up to 460 or 470 in the near-to-medium term; don't ignore it.

mentions.note: A reflection of the Indian capital market; definitely hold for the extreme long term.
mentions.detail: LIC is stabilising at 440 after the ex-bonus. Since listing it gave a good selling opportunity only a couple of times, including above 1,000. More or less LIC is a reflection of the Indian capital market, because LIC itself is the biggest investor in it - a government entity and the largest domestic institutional player. For the extreme long term he would definitely say hold; at current levels, definitely hold without any doubt.

mentions.note: Hold despite disappointing result; GoI EV-bus orders supportive
mentions.detail: The result disappointed and the company keeps failing to complete its expansion, which is a big question. He hasn't been in touch recently and will update next week, but says EV-bus orders from the Government of India are being released proactively. So hold for now.

mentions.note: Mild bottom-fishing only; oil crisis could lift EV demand
mentions.detail: He noted ongoing execution and repeated CEO-change issues and a lack of expected company news. But one advantage now is that the oil crisis could make EV demand jump, so EV companies may perform a bit better. A bottom-fishing attempt could pay off, but only in mild quantity.

mentions.note: Preferred EV play; rose well over the past month; buy EV + renewables now.
mentions.detail: We've been saying for many days, buy Olectra; it rose well over the last one month. There's no other distinct EV stock to buy - if you ask me, combine EV plus renewables and buy now. Adani Power and such have already risen well.

mentions.note: Profits rising YoY (139cr->180cr) but already a multibagger; treat as compounder, add on dips, book some on rises.
Multibagger / 2-3 bagger potential; capacity expansion, electric trucks, BYD battery tie-up, old-bus replacement.
mentions.detail: Sees more than 15-20%/yr - multibagger returns; from here a likely 2-3 bagger. Capacity expansion underway, entering electric trucks, BYD battery tie-up, and India's hard line on China plus old-bus/vehicle replacement (Delhi, Mumbai) are tailwinds. Picked it first from ~90.

mentions.note: Bullish if it delivers on timeline/capacity ramp; BYD battery tie-up and localisation positive, but competition rising.

mentions.price: long term near 1,275-1,300; momentum only above 1,450-1,460
mentions.note: Buy after a 5-10% correction; it plays a band between ~1,250 and ~1,470.
mentions.detail: Olectra is a buy, though not immediately - buy after a 5-10% correction from here. The stock plays continuously in a band: it comes close to 1,250-1,240, then goes again to 1,460-1,470 and faces resistance there. If playing for momentum, buy only after 1,450-1,460 is crossed this time. If buying for the long term, buy only when it comes closer to 1,275-1,280, that is, once it comes near 1,300.

mentions.note: Momentum in a generics player; hold, but keep a trading stop into results.
mentions.detail: Akums Drugs recovered recently, reaching 700 from 450-460. They are basically a generic player rather than a specialty-drugs company, and in generics they have notched up on good turnovers. As of now hold, since there is momentum. But if bought for the short term, definitely keep a trading stop loss, because it is result season and any disappointment in the result can correct the stock immediately.

mentions.note: Buy for the long term; wait for the result, then start accumulating.
mentions.detail: He would buy PPL Pharma for the long term. In the short term there may not be great returns and the stock is still in consolidation mode. Wait for the result too - the last result was very disappointing, but this time result expectations are good. If a somewhat better result comes, start accumulating then.

mentions.note: Speculative bet post block deal; SL 110
mentions.detail: After the ~Rs 8,000 cr block deal, he noted last time the stock ran from below 100 to 150 over six months. It is a high-PE stock but the placement was done successfully. As a speculative bet, buying with a stop loss at 110 could give returns; watch if the post-block-deal pattern repeats.

mentions.note: Accumulate when it comes close to 100; will emerge as a stronger stock but a hype/80-PE stock, not cheap.
mentions.detail: Will emerge as one of the stronger stocks (bounces from lows even post-QIP; good turnover/profitability vs other retailers). Hype stock, not cheap (80 PE); accumulate close to 100.

mentions.note: Best of the times are over; large retail gives steady gains but no spectacular wealth.
mentions.detail: Asked about buying for the long term (the caller noted falling promoter stake and rising FII/DII stakes), he said the best of the times are over in it. The stock corrected close to 100, then rose after the regular equity divestment to almost 130-140, and has come back into consolidation at 117-118. In the extreme long term, unless large retail companies also diversify well on the quick-commerce side and sustain it, they can give steady gains but there is no scene of them creating spectacular wealth - be it DMart or Vishal Mega Mart.

mentions.note: Good promoter, corrected a lot and attractive now; accumulate slowly but check the result.
mentions.detail: KP Energy is a buy because it is a good promoter. The stock has corrected a lot in recent times and is attractive right now. On the indicators the bottom is done at 250 and it is slowly inching up. Start accumulating slowly, but wait for and look at the result once. 'Buy - and definitely KP Energy.'

mentions.note: A huge wealth-creating stock; definitely buy for the long term only.
mentions.detail: Asked whether one can buy at the current market price, he called Vinati Organics a huge wealth-creating stock. But it is for the long term - definitely not for the short term. For the long term, definitely buy.

mentions.note: Book partial profits; keep a trailing stop on the rest while momentum lasts.
mentions.detail: A recently listed company (from the IPO) that languished for many days before a spike: from about 120 at March-ending, the stock doubled to 200-plus in just the April-May-June quarter. He said booking partial profits here is also advisable. For the rest, keep a trailing stop loss and hold, since there is momentum in the stock as of now. (The stock name was garbled in the captions and is flagged as heard.)

mentions.note: Hold; realty under pressure from IT job-loss risk
mentions.detail: Realty stocks are under pressure, and metro-centric realty like Brigade (Hyderabad/Bangalore) could be hit if IT weakness brings job losses; Brigade and Prestige are connected to that risk. But as of now he says hold.

mentions.note: Hold; reduce on a technical rally near 800
mentions.detail: Realty stocks are weak now, but Brigade has the advantage of regular lease income apart from construction income. The stock is at low levels, so no need to sell here. On any technical rally closer to 800, reduce the position. Hold as of now.

mentions.note: In a downtrend but attractive zone, below long-term averages; hold for now and exit if rallies come.

Has given an uptick; almost all realty stocks have been consolidating for six months. With the RBI keeping interest rates steady, real estate can recover in Q2-Q4 depending on rates. Hold for the present (caller at a 30% loss asked about averaging).
Low-beta stock; hold for now.
Don't switch out; largest private bank has to perform eventually
mentions.detail: Caller has been stuck for three years and asked about switching out. Kutumba Rao says don't shift and keep it. It is certainly an underperformer, no doubt, but being the largest private bank it has to perform at some point, so hold.

mentions.note: Bought himself today on cheap valuation
mentions.detail: Kutumba Rao said he bought HDFC Bank today, around Rs 795 (it came twice), purely on valuation - a rare ~15.4 PE and price-to-book of ~2.6. He noted management is confident, with interim chairman KK Mistry and MD Sashidhar Jagdishan giving positive assurances after the boardroom resignation.

mentions.note: Value-unlocking signs (HDFC Sec, AMC, Life); at not even 11-12 PE, will attract buyers.
mentions.detail: Largest private bank, competitively placed; value-unlocking signs given subsidiaries (HDFC Securities, HDFC AMC) and already-divested HDFC Life. Including investment value it's available at not even 11-12 PE; will attract buyers at some point.

mentions.note: FIIs sold over Rs 2,50,000 crore in 2 years after peak ~85% holding; once FII money flows back it can outperform - hold.
Can be accumulated slowly from this range; momentum has been coming into banking and financial stocks for the last few days.
Contrarian buy; good management, fundamentals and nationwide franchise.
mentions.detail: For a caller seeking a good private bank (already holding RBL and Federal), Kutumba Rao recommended Kotak contrarian-wise. Good management moving ahead with good fundamentals, a strong nationwide network and an excellent franchise.

mentions.note: Among the three: high-beta private bank, may or may not work out, a bit risky
Same as HDFC Bank - accumulate slowly from this range on the back of the recent momentum in banking and financials.
Buy only with a long-term view
mentions.detail: Near a 52-week low and a sin stock the government keeps eyeing. Even as a diversified FMCG company, he said buy only if you have a long-term view; in a short-term position don't expect big returns.

mentions.note: Good company; hold, watch under-ownership factor
mentions.detail: Part of viewer Satish Rao's basket sitting at 25-40% losses. Kutumba Rao views these as fundamentally good companies; keep modest expectations and focus on the under-ownership factor that drives quick recoveries. A good bet.

mentions.note: Only for extreme long term; accumulate staggered. Has disappointed/corrected over decades.
mentions.detail: Only for the extreme long term (children's-money horizon); accumulate in a staggered manner. ITC has disappointed over 30-40 yrs and has corrected. Not for 1-3 months.

mentions.note: Hold.
mentions.detail: HOLD.

mentions.note: Weak short-term post ex-ITC Hotels, near 52wk low ~289; hold with SL 275, exit on rebound to 320-330.

Somewhat in a downtrend with medium-to-short-term pressure from rising GST, but it averages 15-17% CAGR in the long term. 'Three years is not long term - 5 to 10 years is.' For a 5-10 year investor there will be cycles in between, but it is a stock to hold on to.
Accumulate for medium term
mentions.detail: Kutumba Rao included Vimta Labs among the three (with NCC and Suzlon) he advised accumulating with a medium-term view. (Said with audio trouble; the anchor relayed the accumulate verdict as Kutumba Rao's.)

mentions.note: Last result flat (not bad); one-of-its-kind, little competition; FMEG turnover rising. Expects a remarkable turnaround.
mentions.detail: Last result wasn't bad, it was flat (top and bottom line flat). I think the stock will definitely do a remarkable turnaround because it's one of its kind with little competition; the fast-moving electrical goods unit's turnover is slowly rising and the company can cross Rs 500 cr turnover next year. Hold a bit longer; don't look only for the result.

mentions.note: Contradicted 'nothing great' - definitely good results; record full-year revenue; attractive after the correction.
mentions.detail: Against the anchor's read that the results were nothing great, he said they were definitely good results - from a flat position it clearly showed about 15% growth, and on a full-year basis the company achieved record revenue for the first time. Growth from such a record-revenue base will be very easy. He said hold, adding that after the recent correction the stock is looking very attractive at current levels.

mentions.note: Buy immediately, accumulate slowly; serious 2-year wait, don't panic on interim corrections.
mentions.detail: Buy immediately / accumulate slowly from here; needs a serious 2-year view, don't panic when corrections come in between.

mentions.note: Attractive valuations; needs to hold 530-540 for sustained interest
mentions.detail: No fresh news, but attractive valuations bring intermittent buying. For sustained interest it must hold above 530-540 for two-three days. Accumulate continuously whenever the stock is below 500.

mentions.note: Hold; average only after Q results, not now
mentions.detail: Caller holds 500 shares at average 650, now ~600, and asked about averaging. Kutumba Rao said don't average now but hold, since it already closed 600-plus recently after testing a low near 400 and is now forming higher top/higher bottom. Consider averaging only after this quarterly result, no need to rush.

mentions.note: Hold (mailer holding around 600). If averaging, do it only after this quarter's results. It is a buy-on-dips kind of stock for the long term.

mentions.detail: Weak results, competition rising in luggage with new brands pulling share, stock near 52-week low. Difficult to regain lost market share unless it reinvents/rebrands. I would avoid it.

mentions.note: Preferred over Tata Motors Passenger Vehicles
mentions.detail: He would prefer M&M over Tata Motors Passenger Vehicles, describing it as a much better stock even if costlier, and would shift from Tata Motors PV to M&M on rallies.

mentions.note: Hold/accumulate; treat as a diversified engineering/defense stock, not just autos
mentions.detail: Asked by a viewer holding M&M at Rs 1700, Kutumba Rao said keep it because M&M also has defense exposure and is a diversified engineering stock, not just automobiles. He said hold, fresh buying is fine, and if asked, he would accumulate.

mentions.note: Buy immediately, at least a 1-year view.
mentions.detail: M&M - buy immediately, at least with a one-year view.

mentions.note: Good last-quarter and full-year results; data-centre/new segments opening up. Add slowly on dips.
mentions.detail: Add slowly on dips. Good last quarter and full-year results. Now caters to data centres / new segments beyond just retail and corporate; markets opening up.

mentions.note: Add slowly on dips; good last-quarter and full-year results; new demand from data centres beyond retail/corporate.
Hold as dark horse with stop-loss; Rs 1L cr funding + SBI package, Birla back in mgmt, but Bharti/Jio pressure.
mentions.detail: Hold (bought at 1.50). Rs 1 lakh crore funding plan over 3 years + Rs 35,000 cr SBI-led package; Kumar Mangalam Birla back in management is positive. But Bharti/Jio competition (Jio IPO ahead) means tough times. Treat as a dark horse with a stop-loss.

mentions.note: Short-term impact only; long term much better; near 52-wk low, negatives discounted. Hold; accumulate ~1,750.
mentions.detail: Short-term impact on market share/product range; long term company will do much better. Already near 52-week low, most negatives discounted. Hold if owned; if buying, start accumulating close up to 1,750 (figure as spoken).

mentions.note: Betting-policy euphoria fizzled; only for high risk-appetite investors.
mentions.detail: Avoid. Stock shows some momentum on the government betting policy/guidelines but the initial euphoria/exuberance has fizzled. Only for those with high risk-appetite and capacity; such business-model stocks not suitable for everyone.

mentions.note: Stay invested; slight recovery path, sustains above 475; no clear rationale to switch to Kaynes.
mentions.detail: Stay invested with Newgen. In a slight recovery path; recently went 500-plus and stays above 475 even after correcting. Doesn't understand the rationale for switching from Newgen to Kaynes.

mentions.note: Hardware/server maker, not an IT software firm
mentions.detail: On Network Technologies/Netweb he stressed it is a hardware company supplying servers and high-end computing solutions (and some software) to IT firms, so it should not be valued like software companies. It took a hit in the recent tech selloff (had gone to ~4,500, back to ~3,600), but results are good and there is no need to panic — hold.

mentions.note: Good selection; start in tranches
mentions.detail: Part of the Polycab/NetWeb/Airtel question. He said the stock selection is good, so start buying little by little in tranches.

mentions.note: His pick of the four; good returns from current levels
mentions.detail: Among the caller's four stocks he would take Netweb. Called it a slightly risky bet but one that should give good returns from current levels. Even at a high PE (~110-112), once recovery kicks in the stock should definitely do much better.

mentions.note: Hold for now; last quarter very strong, if replicated price sustains with chance of more upside.
Second choice; accumulate on every dip along with MTAR.
mentions.detail: Second choice after MTAR. Accumulate nicely on every dip, along with MTAR.

mentions.note: Second pick among the four; accumulate nicely on every dip.
Decent March-quarter and full-year results; add slowly on declines around 2,500, Rs 100 below, staggered.
mentions.detail: Add slowly on declines in the 2,500 range. Decent March quarter and full-year results. Around 2,500 and Rs 100 below, in a staggered manner on dips.

mentions.note: A bit weakish currently but hold.
mentions.detail: Currently a bit weakish, but still hold.

mentions.note: Avoid until clarity; fertilizer crisis hurts imported-ore converters
mentions.detail: He said it is unfavourable because India mostly imports phosphate ore and then converts it, so even this resilient stock has started falling sharply, sliding from a stable Rs 130-140 in February to about Rs 110. Kharif season is starting and a good monsoon could help, but an uneven monsoon would hit fertilizer usage. As of now he would avoid until stability and clarity come.

mentions.note: Fell ~50% from high; fertiliser imports tough; better only if raw-material sourcing right, else likely stays weak.
mentions.detail: Fell by half from its high. Fertiliser imports getting tough. Would perform better only if raw-material sourcing is correct; otherwise likely stays weak. Phosphate is largely imported, processed here and sold.

mentions.note: Doubts for a 2-3 month bet; profit more than halved; needs deeper study, don't buy just because it fell.
mentions.detail: Has his own doubts for a 2-3 month bet. Fell 20%; profit dropped more than half (top line held). Needs deeper study whether one-off or sustained. Don't buy just because it fell; could fall further.

mentions.note: Buy in the offer-for-sale at the lowest point
mentions.detail: Buy in the offer-for-sale opening today at Rs 104 (5% discount to market), as it's the lowest point. Value is around Rs 100, and at ~20 PE it becomes an attractive valuation. It is a simple, steady finance company with a moat, although it was earlier over-hyped (rose from Rs 22-25 to 150-160 in the railway-stock frenzy).

mentions.note: Hold for dividend; 175 far off
mentions.detail: Said enjoy the dividend and keep holding; the rate of 175 rupees won't come soon, it has to come someday far off.

mentions.price: around Rs 80 would be better; below OFS price
mentions.note: Buy only for the dividend; weak optimism, trading below OFS price
mentions.detail: Optimism is weak; even the recent OFS got no response, and it trades about 7-8% below the OFS price. Railway stocks have become dividend stocks. If it comes near Rs 80 it would be better; watch the March-ending result, and if it sustains the company can report around Rs 7,000 crore-plus net profit and possibly raise the dividend. Buy only for that dividend; otherwise nothing else.

mentions.note: Hold temporarily; weak now but stable March results and dividend
mentions.detail: Weak as of now but the March results were stable and there is a dividend. No need to sell in a hurry in this weakness. If you must sell, sell ex-dividend.

mentions.note: Exit on rallies; weak
mentions.detail: Weak as of now. Exit on rallies.

mentions.note: Solar theme has momentum but mind entry prices and margin of safety; currently ~425; if 500-550 comes book some profit and run profits.
Down in the recent midcap correction but a decent name; hold.
Average; earnings-call developments could take it past 500
mentions.detail: Average here. There are a couple of developments in the company that were indicated on the earnings/conference call. If those play out, there are signs the stock will cross 500 again in the next two-to-three quarters.

mentions.note: Hold; decent March results, can recover to 480-500
mentions.detail: Temporarily weak but decent enough March results. If the market gets some stability, KPI Green should improve again with clear signs of moving closer to 480-500. No need to panic.

mentions.note: Exit/reduce on rallies; peak reached, flat result
mentions.detail: The stock has reached a peak; it touched over 1,100 and is forming lower tops and lower bottoms, and the result was flat. It already ran from ~500 to 700-plus recently, so it is better to reduce the position. Exit on rallies.

mentions.note: US-India trade-deal overhang impacts revenues medium/short term after capacity expansion; hold for now
Exit at 180 on rallies for re-entry lower

The stock has likely bottomed, but a rally from here depends on cotton prices. Exit on rallies at 180 for a re-entry at lower levels.

mentions.note: Result okay-not-great; back in buying range
mentions.detail: Yesterday's result wasn't great but is okay. The renewable space gets on-and-off interest and the stock has come below 50, into the buying range. Hold.

mentions.note: Buy with patience; good valuation, renewables demand strong
mentions.detail: Definitely buy at a decent valuation; results came with top line sustained and ~Rs 300 cr profit even after removing a one-off. Renewable space has continuous demand, so no fear. Stock will consolidate in the 48-50 range and rise slowly given high float (promoter only ~12%, public+institutions ~88%); a decisive cross of 54-55 can bring a big move.

mentions.note: Stagnant near 50 on institutional supply
mentions.detail: He said hold Suzlon at current levels. Due to institutional supply the stock has stagnated around 50 and needs a breakout from there before it can move higher.

mentions.note: Buy with medium-term view; recent correction complete, can hold above 50.
mentions.detail: Definitely buy if you have a medium-term view; recent correction is complete, stock can stay steady above 50.

mentions.note: Hold without doubt; stabilizing above 50
mentions.detail: Interest will return to it as the range opens up. The stock is now stabilizing above 50, in the 54-55 range. Hold, without any doubt.

mentions.note: Exit on any rally to 1,000-plus; continuous weakness
mentions.detail: The stock has continuously fallen and displayed weakness even after results. It failed to cross the previous high of 1,125 (and the ~1,300 high), and fizzled out near 1,100. It is already at 925 with 1,000 breaking; on any rally to 1,000-plus, definitely exit. It could stay flat for a few days.

mentions.note: Buy as market consolidates, with a strict stop loss near 500
mentions.detail: Its earlier rise was on data-center hype; it went above 700 then corrected, and now shows some stability. Last result commentary said projects are on track, bringing renewed buying interest. It dipped below 500 over three months and has risen to about 550; as the market consolidates one can buy, but keep a strict stop loss around 500.

mentions.note: Don't sell at current levels; data-centre story, now at a decent valuation after recent volatility.
mentions.detail: At current levels you shouldn't sell, because recently the stock has seen a lot of ups and downs; positive on the data-centre story, and it is now available at a decent valuation (~436).

mentions.note: Hold; good fundamentals but won't outperform now
mentions.detail: A good stock with good fundamentals, but not one that can outperform the market at this time. It has the financial strength to perform over a period. Hold, but do not panic-sell in weakness.

mentions.note: Wait for results (~29th this month) before buying
mentions.detail: One of the good stocks, trading near a 52-week high, though expensive and not a high-volume name; it continues with stable results. Results are nearby (date ~29th this month), so look after results before buying.

mentions.note: His Dec investment idea; results today key to justify the sharp rally
mentions.detail: Kutumba Rao said he has more interest in MTR, which rose very sharply recently, because they have been promoting it as an investment idea since December. He said its results today are key to see whether the rally was justified, so he flagged it to watch around the result.

mentions.note: Terrific result, profit up over 200%, pan-India presence; might jump
mentions.detail: Kutumba Rao noted Satin Creditcare, which he had mentioned earlier, delivered a terrific result with profit jumping over 200% and a pan-India presence. He said the stock might jump, framing it as part of staying invested in fundamentally sound, cash-rich stocks.

mentions.note: EV/energy-transition theme; possible sustained mid-term rally
mentions.detail: Kutumba Rao flagged that companies like JBM Auto and Olectra may have a sustained mid-term rally because of the emphasis the PM placed on the EV space. He said if the crude-oil crisis continues, demand will lean toward EV vehicles and EV public-transport buses, making it a theme to follow carefully.

mentions.note: Hold; possible surprise move as investment-banker peers jump.
mentions.detail: Hold definitely; after the recent correction the stock is looking good again. Investment-banker companies are all jumping, so JM could too. Corrected from near 200 and is now stabilising near 140; if it crosses 150 levels a good move could come.

mentions.note: Coal India subsidiary; only one result so far, definitely hold.
mentions.detail: This is a recently listed company, a subsidiary related to Coal India. Only one result has come so far, so it must definitely be held.

mentions.note: Not very positive on agro-chemicals now; monsoon/El Nino risk.
mentions.detail: Agro-chemical companies with higher raw-material costs will see margins fall; companies with lower raw-material costs will rise. This quarter's performance could be weak because of monsoon and the El Nino effect. So not very positive on agro-chemical companies as of now.

mentions.note: Weak, making losses; don't average, wait for turnaround.
mentions.detail: We're tracking it, but the stock is weak - a chemical-space company that, after re-listing, saw institutional activity rise then fade, and it is now making losses too. Don't average; wait for a turnaround in performance. It's a large company (almost a couple of thousand crores turnover).

mentions.note: Not at current price near 52-week high; pick it up on a fall below 700.
mentions.detail: Not right away - buy Granules when a bit of a decline comes, since it has run to its 52-week highs. It was at a take-off stage around 600 and after a long consolidation broke out there, adding about 150 since. Technically he thinks a fall below 700 should come, and on that correction he would definitely pick it up. The second generation of management is doing extremely well and the shift to a younger management may bring changes; market cap is now close to Rs 20,000 crore. He called it a silent performer that Hyderabad-pharma coverage under-plays versus SMS, Laurus, Natco and Dr Reddy's.

mentions.note: Stable Murugappa Group stock; no excitement but stable returns - medium-to-long term only.
mentions.detail: Carborundum is a stable stock from the Murugappa Group. There is no excitement in it, but there will be stable returns, because in the engineering space there are only two or three stocks of this kind. He said definitely buy, but only for medium-to-long-term stable returns.

mentions.note: Not in favour; work there and take the salary, but invest that money in some other company.
mentions.detail: Asked by an EPAM software engineer whether to continue investing via a 15% ESPP discount, he said he is not in favour of investing in EPAM. He repeated his workshop principle: whatever industry we work in, we know it better than outsiders, so we should rationalise our investments with that knowledge. The mailer already knows his company is an underperformer, which is why he is asking for a second opinion. Work in EPAM, take the salary there, and invest that money in some other company.

mentions.note: Wind/alternative-energy stocks in a trading-range consolidation; valuations attractive, debt reduced (positive); 190-200 is crucial; wait 1-2 quarters, break above 200 opens a rally.
